calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition To Increase Petitioner'S Spousal Monthly Income Allowance And For Order Of Support For Petitioner From Community Property
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg & Will Doyle for (petitioner) Celia Sotiriou.

The Court stated that this is a petition to increase income allowance, that it raises issues, that the premise of the petition is talking about community assets, that it is not clear that Mr. Sotiriou's social security is premised on the 45 years prior to marriage, that it is not sure that the premise of the petition is correct, that there is no evidence of the standard of living during the marriage, asked why the conservatee's cell phone is still being paid for if he is not able to use it, that there is a $600.00 request per month for clothing and toiletries, that the legal fees and medical costs will be paid off and should not be part of the long term budget, that there is a request for $150.00 for house cleaning, that it is not a huge house and the wife is still able to work, that there is an issue of the retroactivity, that it is not clear what the authority is, that the state has already paid the facility and that the facility is not a party to this. Ms. Friedeberg responded to the deficit reduction, and that it is their experience that they can go to Medi-Cal regarding retroactivity with a court order, that the laws are there for a good reason, that they are talking about the share of costs being reduced, that they are asking for it to be retroactive to the date they applied for Medi-Cal, that Medi-Cal has honored this in the past but they are unable to find authority on it, cited 3100(b) & 3102(f) that they are not asking for a transfer, that they are asking the court to set an allowance, that they believe he earned enough credits and that if he predeceases her she will be entitled to his benefit as it is higher. Ms. Friedeberg further stated that they will check into the cell phone cancellation policy, that Ms. Sotiriou has health issues, that she fell during the time of the petition, that her diabetes is progressing and she has limited mobility, that they have asked for less than their budgeted standard of living, that only the grocery bill decreased when Mr. Sotiriou went into the facility, that Medi-Cal does not cover everything and that in regards to the legal and medical expenses it is a one time expense but it is reasonable to expect that there will be other charges. The Court stated that it will take the petition to increase petitioner's spousal monthly income allowance, etc., under submission, that the order will include the determination that these have been her expenses but that it will not order for it to be retroactive.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
